title: Circuit Breaker
description: Stop calls to failing dependencies; half-open probing; fallback. Prevents cascading failures.

cost.costNotes: Seeded via heuristics; validate against current pricing and expected workload (throughput, storage, retention, egress).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Circuit Breaker is a software design pattern implemented in application code, not an infrastructure service. It's a resilience pattern that wraps service calls with state management (closed/open/half-open). Implementation can be via lightweight libraries (Resilience4j, Polly, Hystrix) or service mesh features (Istio, Linkerd) that run within existing infrastructure. While service mesh adds ~5-15ms latency and ~50MB memory per sidecar, this doesn't create new billable infrastructure. Any costs are absorbed by existing compute resources (containers, VMs). The pattern itself adds negligible CPU/memory overhead (<1% typically) for state tracking and timing logic.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity implementation. Key cost factors: (1) Learning curve - understanding state transitions, timeout configurations, failure thresholds, and half-open probe logic (8-16 hours); (2) Implementation - integrating library/framework into existing services, wrapping critical dependencies, configuring thresholds per service type (16-24 hours); (3) Testing - simulating failure scenarios, validating state transitions, testing fallback behaviors, load testing impact (16-24 hours); (4) Observability - adding metrics/dashboards for circuit state, failure rates, latency (8-12 hours). Total ~48-76 engineering hours. With AI coding assistants reducing boilerplate by 30-40% and providing configuration templates, estimate 40-50 hours at $50-75/hour blended rate = $2,000-3,750. Conservative mid-point accounts for pre-built libraries significantly reducing complexity vs custom implementation.
provenance.licenseCost: All major circuit breaker implementations are open source with permissive licenses: Resilience4j (Apache 2.0), Polly (.NET, BSD), Hystrix (Apache 2.0, now maintenance mode), Spring Cloud Circuit Breaker (Apache 2.0), service mesh options (Istio/Envoy Apache 2.0, Linkerd Apache 2.0). No commercial licensing required for the pattern itself. Enterprise support contracts optional but not necessary for implementation (e.g., Buoyant for Linkerd $5k-50k/year, but pattern works without it). Organizations can implement completely free using open source libraries.

provides.reasoning: The circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures by tripping open when a dependency fails repeatedly, stopping calls until it recovers.
Circuit breaker isolates failing dependencies, preventing them from impacting the rest of the system. This is a core capability that should be explicitly listed.
The pattern's fallback mechanism enables the system to continue operating in a degraded state rather than completely failing, which is a key resilience capability.

requires.reasoning: Circuit breakers need timeout mechanisms to detect hanging calls and unresponsive services. Without timeouts, the circuit breaker cannot determine when to trip and would wait indefinitely.
The pattern requires a way to detect and count failures (e.g., error responses, exceptions) to determine when to open the circuit. This is fundamental to the pattern's operation.
Circuit breakers need to track success/failure rates, response times, and call volumes to make informed decisions about circuit state transitions (closed -> open -> half-open).

supports_nfr.reason: Circuit breaker adds complexity and operational overhead. At availability targets below 99%, simpler error handling may suffice.
Circuit breaker adds minimal latency overhead (state check) in closed state, typically sub-millisecond.
Circuit breaker state transitions and fallback logic introduce minimal latency overhead in tail cases.
Circuit breaker enables rapid recovery through half-open state probing, typically achieving sub-minute to 1-minute RTO.
Circuit breaker state management and monitoring add overhead. At very low QPS, simpler retry logic may be more appropriate.
Pattern is GDPR-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is CCPA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling in the application layer, not this pattern.
Pattern is HIPAA-agnostic; compliance depends on data handling and provider BAAs, not this pattern.
Pattern is SOX-agnostic; compliance depends on audit controls in the application layer, not this pattern.

supports_constraints.reason: Circuit breaker is a cloud-agnostic resilience pattern applicable to any deployment environment.
Circuit breaker is a design pattern that can be implemented in any programming language with libraries or custom code.
Circuit breaker pattern applies to any platform that makes external calls including APIs, workers, batch jobs, and pipelines.
Circuit breaker is compatible with both cached and non-cached architectures.
Circuit breaker applies to both synchronous request-response and asynchronous messaging patterns.
Circuit breaker is useful for protecting AI inference endpoints but does not require AI inference to be present.
Circuit breaker can be applied in both single-tenant and multi-tenant architectures with per-tenant or global configuration.
Circuit breaker can protect batch job dependencies and external service calls in batch processing workflows.
Circuit breaker is compatible with both distributed transaction and non-transactional architectures.
Circuit breaker can protect streaming pipeline dependencies but does not require real-time streaming to be present.
Circuit breaker can protect vector search service calls but does not require or exclude vector search capabilities.
Circuit breaker can protect document store calls but does not require or exclude document database usage.
Circuit breaker can protect key-value store calls but does not require or exclude key-value database usage.
Circuit breaker can protect graph database calls but does not require or exclude graph database usage.
Circuit breaker can protect time-series database calls but does not require or exclude time-series database usage.
Circuit breaker is beneficial for protecting OLTP workloads from cascading failures but works with all workload types.
Circuit breaker can protect OLAP query dependencies but does not require or exclude analytical workloads.

failure_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before opening the circuit. Lower values trip faster but may be more sensitive to transient errors; higher values are more tolerant but slower to protect.

success_threshold.description: Number of consecutive successes in half-open state before closing the circuit. Higher values ensure more confidence before resuming normal operation.

timeout_duration.description: Seconds to wait in open state before transitioning to half-open. Shorter timeouts probe recovery faster; longer timeouts give failing services more recovery time.

half_open_max_calls.description: Maximum concurrent requests allowed in half-open state. Use 1 for cautious probing, higher values for faster traffic ramp-up.

fallback_strategy.description: Behavior when circuit is open. fail_fast: immediate error; cached_response: return stale data; default_value: return predetermined fallback; alternative_service: route to backup dependency.
